Red Flag Description: Suspicious pattern emerges from client's transactions (e.g. same time of day)
Atypical transfers by client on in-and-out basis, or cash deposit followed immediately by wire transfer out
Funds transferred in and out of account on same day or within relatively short period
Client conducts transactions at different physical locations
Large and/or rapid movement of funds not commensurate with client's financial profile
Rounded sum transactions atypical of what would be expected from client
Multiple cash deposits below $10,000 within short timeframe to avoid CTR filing requirements
Multiple transactions conducted below reporting threshold within short period
Inactive account begins to see financial activity (deposits, wire transfers, withdrawals)
Accounts receive relevant periodical deposits and are inactive at other periods without logical explanation
Sudden increase in credit card usage or applications for new credit
Abrupt change in account activity
Credit card transactions and payments exceptionally high including excessive cash advances, balance transfers, or luxury items
Transactional activity far exceeds projected activity at account opening or relationship beginning
Transactional activity (level/volume) inconsistent with client's apparent financial standing, usual pattern, or occupation (student, unemployed, social assistance)
Volume of transactional activity exceeds norm for geographical area
Notes: May indicate hub for ML activity

Red Flag Description: Client appears to be living beyond their means; significantly more spending than income
Size or type of transactions atypical of what is expected from client
Sudden change in client's financial profile, pattern of activity or transactions
Gatekeeper utilizing their account for transactions not typical of their business (pass-through, excessive cash, payments to non-clients)
Entity has business activities or a model that is outside the norm for its sector.
Notes: Profile mismatch in TBML context; trade volume or counterparty mix inconsistent with stated business.

Red Flag Description: The entity transacts with a large number of entities in high-volume, high-demand sectors or unrelated sectors.
Notes: Counterparty in FATF-listed or high-risk jurisdiction without clear trade rationale.

Red Flag Description: Entity receives a sudden inflow of large-value electronic funds transfers.
Notes: Sudden volume spike in trade accounts is a primary TBML trigger; assess against industry seasonality.

Red Flag Description: Orders or receives payments for goods in round figures or in increments of approximately US$50,000.
Notes: Round sum invoices in trade context indicate fictitious or padded invoicing rather than genuine commercial transactions.

Red Flag Description: Transactional activity inconsistent with declared business (e.g. no payrolls, invoices, normal business transactions)
Transactions with jurisdictions known to produce or transit drugs or precursor chemicals, or sources of other criminality
Geographic Focus: Mexico, Colombia, Afghanistan, Golden Triangle, China (precursors)

Red Flag Description: Transactions with jurisdictions known to be at higher risk of ML/TF
Transaction/business activity involving locations of concern (ongoing conflicts, weak ML/TF controls, secretive banking laws)
Geographic Focus: Conflict Zones, Offshore Centers, Sanctioned Countries

Red Flag Description: Transactions involving countries deemed high risk or non-cooperative by FATF
Client makes frequent overseas transfers not in line with their financial profile
Large wire transfers or high volume through account that doesn't fit expected pattern
Client sending to or receiving wire transfers from multiple clients
Rounded sum purchases at grocery stores and/or other retailers that sell gift cards and/or prepaid credit cards.
Notes: Gift card purchases in rounded sums are a common trafficking proceeds conversion technique; often combined with ATM withdrawals.

Red Flag Description: Atypical high-value purchases at convenience stores (likely for gift cards or money transfers).
Notes: Convenience store outlier spend indicates likely gift card or prepaid instrument purchase; assess frequency alongside other HT indicators.

Red Flag Description: Luxury purchases (dealerships, high-end restaurants, jewelry) inconsistent with reported income or occupation.
Notes: Luxury spend disproportionate to income is a primary proceeds-integration indicator in sex trafficking operations.

Red Flag Description: Frequent low-value payments for parking (indicative of victim transit or recruitment activity).
Notes: Unusually frequent parking spend in urban centers is associated with victim transport and client-facing activity; assess alongside hotel and food delivery spend.

Red Flag Description: Frequent purchases for food delivery services (often multiple times per day).
Notes: Multiple daily food delivery orders may indicate victims being kept in controlled locations; strongest signal when combined with hotel or short-term rental spend.

Red Flag Description: Transfers to virtual currency, online gambling, or investments.
Notes: Digital layering of trafficking proceeds through crypto and gambling platforms; online payment processors used to obscure illicit origin.

Red Flag Description: Location of accommodation bookings corresponds to location of cash deposits across multiple cities.
Notes: Cash deposit city matching hotel booking city across multiple locations is a high-confidence trafficking mobility indicator; corroborates victim movement.

Red Flag Description: Payments to online accommodation or travel websites in non-residential cities (Expedia, Airbnb).
Notes: Frequent out-of-city accommodation bookings without corresponding business travel profile; most significant when combined with cash deposits and food delivery spend.

Red Flag Description: Large international travel purchases (airfare) targeting high-risk source countries (e.g. China, South Korea).
Notes: International airfare to known source countries for trafficking victims; especially significant when travel is repetitive and combined with accommodation and cash spend at destination.

Red Flag Description: Merchant POS transactions (Spa, Massage, Escort) occur after establishment business hours (10 PM to 6 AM).
Notes: After-hours POS activity at massage or spa establishments is a high-confidence illicit operations indicator; legitimate businesses rarely process client payments past midnight.

Red Flag Description: Multiple real estate purchases or high-value transfers disproportionate to reported income.
Notes: Real estate used to house victims and launder proceeds simultaneously; disproportionate property acquisition relative to income is a dual trafficking/ML signal.

Red Flag Description: Monthly payments to multiple individuals or entities involved in residential rentals.
Notes: Multiple rental payments to distinct parties indicate a network of victim accommodation sites; frequency and geographic spread of rental payments are key escalation signals.
